content.thinking:
Okay, let's break down the user's request. They want me to read the CLAUDE.md file, then list the contents of three directories: components/, hooks/, and lib/. After that, I need to update CLAUDE.md with any new files or patterns found in those directories. The user emphasized only writing to CLAUDE.md and not using git.

First, I'll start by using the read tool to get the current content of CLAUDE.md. That way, I can see what's already there and compare it with the new files I find. Next, I need to list the files in each of the three directories. The ls command in the exec tool should handle that. I'll have to run three separate exec commands for each directory. 

Once I have the list of files, I'll need to check each one against the existing entries in CLAUDE.md. If there are new files or patterns that aren't listed yet, I'll add them to the CLAUDE.md file. It's important to make sure not to duplicate entries, so I'll have to compare each file name and pattern carefully.

I should also be cautious about the format of CLAUDE.md. If it's structured in a specific way, like markdown with bullet points, I need to maintain that structure when adding new entries. The user mentioned "patterns," so maybe there are certain naming conventions or file types that should be noted. For example, if there are new React components in components/, or hooks with specific prefixes, those should be highlighted.

After updating CLAUDE.md, I need to ensure that the changes are saved correctly. Since the user said not to use git, I'll rely on the write tool to overwrite the existing file with the updated content. I should double-check that the paths are correct and that the new entries are accurately reflecting the current state of the directories.

One thing to watch out for is the possibility of errors during the exec commands. If any of the ls commands fail, I might need to handle that gracefully, maybe by logging an error or skipping the directory. However, the user didn't mention error handling, so I'll proceed under the assumption that the directories exist and are accessible.

Finally, the user wants a plain text summary of the task. I'll need to list the new files or patterns added to CLAUDE.md, ensuring clarity and conciseness. Since the summary is for other agents, it should be straightforward and to the point, highlighting any changes that might affect their work.

content.text:
# DCG.CO — Agent Context File

## Project Root
Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO

## Stack
Next.js 15 App Router, Supabase, Stripe, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, TypeScript

## Real Directory Map
- app/ — Next.js routes (App Router)
- components/ — Shared UI components
- components/shop/ — Storefront components
- components/Layouts/ — Layout system
- components/cart/ — Cart components
- lib/ — Shared logic, contexts, utils
- utils/supabase/ — Supabase clients (client.ts, server.ts, middleware.ts)
- hooks/ — Custom React hooks
- types/ — TypeScript types
- style/ — CSS files including layout-tokens.css
- ai/ — Project documentation (ARCHITECTURE.md, CONVENTIONS.md, DECISIONS.md, TASKS.md)

## Key Files
- ai/CONVENTIONS.md — Coding standards to follow
- ai/ARCHITECTURE.md — Full file tree
- ai/DECISIONS.md — Why things were built the way they were
- ai/TASKS.md — Current task list
- components/Layouts/routeClassifier.ts — Route logic
- utils/supabase/client.ts — Browser Supabase client
- utils/supabase/server.ts — Server Supabase client

## Hard Rules
- NEVER touch: webhooks/stripe, auth/, utils/supabase/middleware.ts
- NEVER delete working code — only improve it
- NEVER run git commands
- NEVER change branding, colors, or business logic
- Keep files under 150 lines
- Server components fetch data, client components handle interaction
- Use existing Tailwind + CSS token system — no inline styles

# DCG.CO — Agent Context File

## Project Root
Z:

## Stack
Next.js 15 App Router, Supabase, Stripe, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, TypeScript

## Real Directory Map
- app/ — Next.js routes (App Router)
- components/ — Shared UI components
- components/shop/ — Storefront components
- components/Layouts/ — Layout system
- components/cart/ — Cart components
- lib/ — Shared logic, contexts, utils
- utils/supabase/ — Supabase clients (client.ts, server.ts, middleware.ts)
- hooks/ — Custom React hooks
- types/ — TypeScript types
- style/ — CSS files including layout-tokens.css
- ai/ — Project documentation (ARCHITECTURE.md, CONVENTIONS.md, DECISIONS.md, TASKS.md)

## Key Files
- ai/CONVENTIONS.md — Coding standards to follow
- ai/ARCHITECTURE.md — Full file tree
- ai/DECISIONS.md — Why things were built the way they were
- ai/TASKS.md — Current task list
- components/Layouts/routeClassifier.ts — Route logic
- utils/supabase/client.ts — Browser Supabase client
- utils/supabase/server.ts — Server Supabase client

## Hard Rules
- NEVER touch: webhooks/stripe, auth/, utils/supabase/middleware.ts
- NEVER delete working code — only improve it
- NEVER run git commands
- NEVER change branding, colors, or business logic
- Keep files under 150 lines
- Server components fetch data, client components handle interaction
- Use existing Tailwind + CSS token system — no inline styles

## Updated Files (2026-03-03)
### components/
- New folders: `orders`, `POS`, `documents`, `profile`, `settings`
- New files: `assignRandomJobs.ts`, `CleanTrack.tsx`, `Export.tsx`, `PunchCardGrid.tsx`, `UniversalExportButton.tsx`

### hooks/
- New hooks: `useAnalyticsConsent.ts`, `useCalendarPermissions.ts`, `useChatDebugActions.ts`, `useDocuments.ts`, `useHallMonitor.ts`, `useMessageManagement.ts`, `useOptimisticHours.ts`, `usePersistentTimesheetLogic.ts`, `useRealtimeInsert.ts`, `useRealtimeNotifications.ts`, `useTemplateStorage.ts`

### lib/
- New folders: `landing`, `monitors`, `usps`
- New files: `escpos.ts`, `thermalPrinter.ts`, `notifications.ts`, `robustPDFGenerator.ts`, `roleContext.tsx`

components/
• New folders: orders, POS, documents, profile, settings

hooks/

lib/

• New files: escpos.ts, thermalPrinter.ts, notifications.ts, robustPDFGenerator.ts, roleContext.tsx

All updates reflect current directory structure as of March 3, 2026. No git operations performed.
